channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News
channel.description: Curated geopolitical headlines from tiered sources

item.title: Russian missile barrage kills 11, sets historic Kyiv cathedral ablaze
item.description: Russia fired missiles across multiple Ukrainian cities, killing at least 11 people and sparking a fire at the 11th-century Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra monastery. Russia denied striking the UNESCO site, blaming Ukrainian air defenses.

item.title: Lukashenko urges both sides to compromise to end Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Belarusian President Lukashenko, one of Putin's closest allies, publicly called for compromise from both Russia and Ukraine to end the war, a notable shift in rhetoric from a key Kremlin ally.

item.title: Oil companies give Venezuela a second look amid Middle East chaos
item.description: Trump's Middle East policy disruptions are creating an unexpected beneficiary: Venezuela. Oil companies experiencing FOMO are reconsidering Venezuelan opportunities as Mideast supply uncertainties persist.

item.title: Senate Republicans demand a say on Trumps Iran deal
item.description: Republican lawmakers said they are eager to examine the fine print of the US-Iran MOU, though there is a high bar to overturning any final agreement. The move signals congressional assertiveness on war powers.

item.title: Trump dismisses reports of $300B Iran reconstruction fund as fake news
item.description: President Trump pushed back on reports that a $300 billion reconstruction fund for Iran would be part of the peace agreement, casting doubt on one of the most controversial rumored elements of the deal.

item.title: B-52 bomber crashes at Edwards Air Force Base, all 8 crew presumed dead
item.description: A US Air Force B-52 Stratofortress crashed shortly after takeoff at Edwards Air Force Base in California during a routine test mission. The base declared the crash "not survivable" and closed the airfield.

item.title: US and Iran sign MOU to end war, formal signing ceremony set for Friday
item.description: Senior US officials confirmed the memorandum of understanding has been signed electronically, with a formal ceremony planned for Friday. Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz will gradually ramp up as mines are cleared.

item.title: France and Britain push multinational naval mission to secure Strait of Hormuz
item.description: France and Britain are advancing plans for a coalition naval mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz if the US-Iran ceasefire holds. It remains unclear whether Tehran would accept such an operation.

item.title: What to know about the US-Iran deal to end the war and reopen Hormuz
item.description: AP breaks down the key elements of the preliminary US-Iran agreement: immediate ceasefire, gradual reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, IAEA inspector return, and a formal signing ceremony expected on Friday with mediation from Pakistan.

item.title: G7 Opens in France as Trump Pushes to End Ukraine War After Iran Deal
item.description: Group of Seven leaders convened in Évian-les-Bains as Trump arrived touting a preliminary deal to end the Iran war and pledging to now seek an end to fighting in Ukraine and Lebanon.

item.title: Zelensky Offers to Meet Putin at G7 or in the US for Peace Talks
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelensky said he offered to meet Putin face-to-face at the G7 summit in France or in the United States, aiming to end the four-year war. Putin has not responded.

item.title: Trump and Iran Dominate G7 Summit Opening Amid Hormuz Disputes
item.description: The Iran deal overshadowed the G7 summit opening. Trump expressed confidence the Strait of Hormuz would reopen by Friday, but allies remained wary of tolls, mines, and enforcement gaps.

item.title: White House Order to Pull Anthropic AI Models Fuels Warning of Ad Hoc Regulation
item.description: The Trump administration forced Anthropic to disable its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models citing security concerns, drawing criticism that US AI regulation remains reactive and inconsistent rather than structured through clear policy.

item.title: Commentary: Why the US Government Shut Down Anthropic's Latest Claude Model
item.description: Channel News Asia analyzes how the US administration shifted from a hands-off AI posture to ordering Anthropic to disable its newest models, raising questions about sovereign AI development and the future of frontier model governance.

item.title: ECB Welcomes Iran Ceasefire Deal But Warns Inflation Relief Will Take Time
item.description: ECB President Christine Lagarde welcomed the US-Iran ceasefire agreement as good news that could help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but cautioned it would not immediately bring down high eurozone inflation. Some ECB colleagues echoed concerns about persistent price pressures.

item.title: Ebola Outbreak Strains Health System in Eastern Congo as Cases Surge
item.description: A new Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo Ituri province is overwhelming an already fragile health system. Hospitals in Bunia are overwhelmed or shut down after suspected infections, while health workers race to contain the virus one month into the outbreak.

item.title: Russia denies striking Kyiv monastery, claims Ukrainian Patriot missile caused damage
item.description: Russia said it did not strike the historic Pechersk Lavra monastery in Kyiv during its overnight attack, instead blaming a U.S.-made Patriot air defence missile for the damage to the nearly 1,000-year-old site.

item.title: Myanmar’s Min Aung Hlaing makes first visit to China as civilian president
item.description: Myanmar’s former junta chief Min Aung Hlaing arrived in Beijing for a five-day visit at Xi Jinping’s invitation, his first trip to China since being elected president — a sign of deepening ties between the two authoritarian governments.

item.title: Ukraine Scrambles for Air Defense Alternatives as Patriot Missiles Run Low
item.description: Russia fired 34 ballistic missiles at Ukraine in a single overnight attack on Kyiv that set the historic Pechersk Lavra ablaze. With Patriot interceptors scarce and increasingly in demand globally, Kyiv is pursuing aging PAC-2/PAC-3 stockpiles and alternative systems to fill the gap.

item.title: Zelensky Proposes Meeting Putin in the US After Russian Leader Ignores G7 Invitation
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelensky said he offered to meet Putin in the United States during a call with Trump, suggesting Putin would find a US meeting difficult to refuse. The proposal came after the Russian leader reportedly ignored a G7 summit invitation.

item.title: UK to Ban Social Media for Under-16s, May Impose Overnight Curfews
item.description: The UK government announced it will ban social media for children under 16, requiring age verification and potentially imposing overnight curfews on platform access. Platforms including TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube are in scope.

item.title: Philippines’ Marcos and Russia’s Putin to Discuss Energy and Food Security
item.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and Russian President Vladimir Putin are set to hold talks focused on energy cooperation and food security, a notable diplomatic engagement as Manila balances ties between Washington and Moscow.

item.title: Initial deal to end US-Iran war moves toward formal signing despite lingering questions
item.description: The US-Iran agreement to extend the ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz is advancing toward formal signing, but critical questions remain about enforcement, Iran's nuclear program, and the role of Israel and regional allies in the broader settlement.

item.title: Russian strike damages historic Kyiv cathedral, kills 11 across Ukraine
item.description: A major Russian overnight barrage of missiles and drones killed at least 11 people across Ukraine and set fire to the nearly 1,000-year-old Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, one of the country's most sacred cultural landmarks. Ukraine's Orthodox leader called Putin "Antichrist" for the attack.

item.title: US and Iran sign framework deal with 60-day ceasefire, leaving major issues for future talks
item.description: The United States and Iran signed an initial framework agreement that includes a 60-day ceasefire to pave the way for negotiations toward a final peace deal and discussions on Iran's nuclear program. Key details on the Strait of Hormuz reopening and sanctions relief remain unresolved.
